title: No-bake strawberry cheesecake
subtitle: A set cream cheese filling on a biscuit base with fresh strawberries
excerpt: A no-bake cheesecake with a digestive biscuit base, a set cream cheese and double cream filling, and fresh strawberries on top. It goes into the fridge rather than the oven and sets firm enough to slice cleanly.
sourceNotes: No-bake cheesecake is a British domestic staple with roots in the American cream-cheese dessert tradition of the 1950s and 1960s, when refrigeration became widespread in homes. The digestive biscuit base is a British adaptation (replacing the American graham cracker). The set filling using whipped double cream rather than gelatine (the American method) is documented in Delia Smith's Complete Cookery Course (1978) and Nigella Lawson's How to Be a Domestic Goddess (2000).

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cheesecake base and filling can be made up to 2 days ahead and kept in the fridge. Add the strawberry topping only on the day of serving.

baking.decoratingTechnique: fresh strawberries halved and arranged over the set filling, brushed with warm jam

content.text: The filling sets through the fat in the double cream and cream cheese firming in the cold. For it to slice cleanly, the cheesecake needs a minimum of 6 hours in the fridge, and overnight is better. A cheesecake that has been chilled for less time will collapse when the tin is released.

Use a springform tin. A fixed-base tin makes it nearly impossible to remove the cheesecake without breaking it. Line the base with a circle of baking paper. The springform release lets the sides drop away cleanly.

Mix the crushed {{digestive-biscuits}} with the melted {{unsalted-butter}} until the crumbs are evenly coated. Press firmly into the base of a 20 cm springform tin in an even layer. Refrigerate for 30 minutes while you make the filling.
Make the filling and set, 6 hours or overnight

Beat the {{cream-cheese}} with the {{icing-sugar}}, {{vanilla-extract}}, and {{lemon-juice}} until smooth. In a separate bowl, whip the cold {{double-cream}} to soft peaks. Fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture in two additions until just combined and no streaks remain. Spoon over the chilled biscuit base and smooth the top. Refrigerate for at least 6 hours or overnight.

Arrange the halved {{strawberries}} over the set filling, cut-side down, starting from the outside edge and working inward. Brush the warmed {{jam}} over the strawberries to give a glossy finish. Release the springform tin and serve immediately or return to the fridge for up to 4 hours.
